violations.description: Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. -Cottage cheese, mandarin oranges, chopped tomatoes and chopped peppers in cookline refrigerator were not date marked. -Portion items (i.e. taco meat, hash browns, etc.) in cook line make table were not date marked. -Buffet items (i.e. potato salad, coleslaw) in back refrigerator were not date marked. -Whole baked potatoes and prime rib in walk in cooler were not date marked.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Dust/debris at top of cook line refrigerator. -Grease/residue at pizza oven front and handle. -Flour/debris build up at underside of handles at 2 door stainless refrigerator.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored, handled, dispensed in a manner to protect from contamination. -Single use trays on top of glass door cooler are not stored inverted.

Employee dressing areas and lockers. — --employees' clothing/possessions were in an area where contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items may occur. -Phone left on table next to prep sink on cook line. -Jacket on top of back prep area table top. COS-both items were removed.
Bare hand contact avoided, single- use gloves, and utensils used (3 pts deducted)
